rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHERINE L. FREEMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition To Approve The Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No petition has been filed to request approval of a final account of the deceased executor's administration (or a final report on waiver of account) and exoneration of the deceased executor's bond. Other issues include: 1) Each specific cash beneficiary must also waive the account, as no 7% interest required by Probate Code section 12003 appears to have been paid. See also section 10954(a)(2). 2) A copy of letters issued to David Long as personal representative of the estate of the Nancy Bass must be filed before his waiver of account can be accepted under section 10954(b)(5). 3) No receipt signed by the successor personal representative to confirm the estate assets on hand is on file. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIA C. TANDY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required for failure to address issues cited by Examiner: 1) The $4,200 rent back was not included as an asset on hand at beginning of supplemental accounting (it was included in the end of account assets on hand in the first accounting). It is also unclear why it is being distributed solely to the administrator, as it appears to be an estate asset. 2) There is a $249.42 reconciliation listed in the liabilities schedule without any additional information. The amount(s) should have been included in the disbursements schedule and a separate reconciliation schedule provided to reconcile the amount with the bank statement. 3) It appears that the estate is not fully FDIC insured. Proof of full FDIC insurance will need to be filed. 4) Revised proposed order is needed. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM MICHAEL HENRY REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Court Approval Of Settlement Agreement And Retention Of Jurisdiction To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/12/2023 (no earlier date available), as the following issues (see tentative ruling for 06/13/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. A copy of the settlement agreement must be attached to the proposed order, with the Court's signature line provided on the last attachment page. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) Donna Echiverri-Beckham's address listed on the proof of service is different from her address previously reported to the Court. 4) The information required by LRSF 14.43(D)(1)-(3) must be provided. 5) CCP section 664.6(a) requires parties to "pending litigation" to stipulate, but the underlying petitions were both taken off calendar at the 02/28/2023 hearing. Do the parties to the settlement agreement qualify as parties to pending litigation entitled to request the Court's retention of jurisdiction to enforce the settlement? 6) Was the petitioner serving as trustee when this petition was filed? If not, please provide the date(s) of the petitioner's resignation as trustee and appointment of Carole Sullivan as successor trustee. 7) The only heir who signed the settlement agreement is Robert Bruce Smith. Did he have authority to bind the other heirs to the terms of the settlement agreement as attorney-in-fact under a power of attorney or otherwise? 8) Should the heirs who are not beneficiaries of the trust and did not sign the settlement agreement be required to be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 17203(b) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq.?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TREVOR KNOOP FIRST PARTY SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/23/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) Item 1 of the DE-120 form incorrectly stated that this is a final account. New notice must be given with the word "final" removed from item 1 of the DE-120 form. 2) Has the beneficiary waived the petitioner's bond? No signed waiver is on file. See LRSF 14.16(H) for required information. 3) No Exhibit A was attached to the supplement to the petition filed on 07/20/2023. A proof of service must also be filed to show a copy of the supplement was served on the person who requested special notice.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DONALD ALLAN KENNEDY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Authorizing And Directing Conservator To Sell Real Property Of The Conservatorship Estate Located At 210-212 Lake St, San Francisco, California 94118 [Apn Lot 021; Block 1355]; And To Enter Into Exclusive Listing Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Contiued to 8/29/2023. Petition alleges that the conservator defers the information to the court appointed attorney; however, pursuant to Prob. Code sec 2540; however, a. Need allegation that the conservator has discussed the proposed sale with the conservatee. b. Need allegation if the conservatee supports or is opposed to the proposed sale. A verified supplement to be filed two weeks prior. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY ELIZABETH TAYLOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/22/23. The following issues to be cleared two weeks prior. (1) Clarification of nature of the asset on I&A. The assets listed appear to be non-cash assets which should have been appraised by the appointed probate referee, not by the personal rep. Prob. Code sec. 8901 and 8902. (2) Declarations regarding XO fee request do not explain why the services subject to XO fees are not part of ordinary services. It appears that Counsel seeks the court approve his services to be paid as extraordinary fees for the amount not covered by statutory compensation. However, much of services seems necessary in due course. If counsel wishes to request fees in relation to a 40-year of statement, declaration needs to provide specific task that would not be a part of ordinary administration pursuant to CRC 7.702. (3) Declaration does not provide why the services provided pre-petition needs to be covered as extraordinary compensation. (4) Proposed distribution to set forth the exact dollar amount to be distributed to each beneficiary after fees. CRC 7.7.651. (5) Verification of cash balance. LRSF 14.34.D and 14.59. (6) Proposed order to be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A. The order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. LRSF 14.35.I.(6). Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF GEORGINA LAXMI LOREK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/22/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/20/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) A statement of each minor's age, health, and whereabouts required by LRSF 14.46(D)(1) must be provided. 3) The beginning account balance does not match the corrected inventory and appraisal on file for each minor's estate. A second corrected inventory and appraisal must be filed to list each minor's right to receive distribution from the probate estate as the only asset on hand at the beginning of this account. 4) The current bond is insufficient to cover the ending cash balance, annual income, and statutory recovery costs for each minor's estate. An ex parte petition must be filed to request an order increasing the bond. The additional bond must be filed before the accounting can be approved. LRSF 14.16(A)(2). 5) Supporting time records (bills) must be provided for the attorneys' fees. LRSF 14.52.
